claim_labels.label: ARGUED
claim_labels.text: Passing relabeling controls is not enough for a source law because the current packet accepts ordered resolution depths as free inputs. The family needs a generator for those depths.

claim_ledger_update: No claim-ledger update is earned. T538 leaves claim rows, Canon Index tiers, and canon verdicts unchanged.

controls.reason: The computed relation is invariant under record and cover relabeling for the nondegenerate fixture.
Renaming cover nodes preserves the relation because the first packet depends only on supplied ordered depths. This passes the shallow isomorphism check but exposes underconstraint.
Total-chain and antichain controls are computed but not promoted into source-law evidence.
Free ordered resolution depths realize several incompatible strict partial-order shapes, so a depth generator is still missing.

not_claimed: T538 does not establish a source law, derive spacetime, prove manifoldlikeness, repair T528, reverse T223, unpause the S1 finite-generator route, promote S1, change claim status, change canon, change public posture, authorize external publication, or move cross-repo truth. It is a Track-1 source-law stress test only.
recipe: Compute x < y from ordered resolution depths by the rule d(x, y) < d(y, x), using only finite record-cover variables.
recommended_next: Run T539 as a resolution-depth generator packet. It must derive ordered depths from local record-cover, restriction-map, and compatible-section data rather than accepting pairwise depths as free inputs. If no nonprogrammatic generator exists, retire this source-law family and re-rank TAF11 against TAF8.
s1_update: S1 remains `requires_added_assumption`. T538 narrows the selected TAF11 family and supplies no S1 evidence.
strongest_reading: The descent-obstruction resolution family survives the shallow relabeling and no-target-import checks, but the first computable form is underconstrained. Free ordered resolution depths can program antichain, total-chain, diamond, and fork relations. That means the current packet does not yet supply a source law.
